docs.title: IsotopeTrack Documentation
docs.text: IsotopeTrack is a free, open-source desktop application for single-particle ICP-ToF-MS (spICP-MS) data analysis. It supports Nu Vitesse and TOFWERK instruments and provides a full graphical pipeline — from raw signal loading to multi-element statistical results.

docs.title: Citation
docs.text:
If you use IsotopeTrack in your research, please cite:

Ahabchane H, Goodman A, Hadioui M, Wilkinson K. IsotopeTrack: A fast and flexible application for the analysis of SP-ICP-TOF-MS datasets. Environmental Chemistry 2026; EN25111. https://doi.org/10.1071/EN25111

docs.title: Downloads
docs.text: Platform Requirements Download macOS — Apple Silicon macOS 11.0 (Big Sur) or later · 4 GB RAM (8 GB recommended) IsotopeTrack_M.dmg Windows Windows 10 (64-bit) or later · 4 GB RAM (8 GB recommended) IsotopeTrack_Setup_W.exe ---

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Multi-isotope single-particle detection
  • Transport rate & ionic calibration (3 methods each)
  • Supports Nu Vitesse folders (run.info), TOFWERK (.h5), and CSV
  • Interactive drag-and-drop results canvas with 16 plot types
  • Batch processing and comprehensive export options
  • Light / dark theme, fully responsive UI

docs.title: Recommended Workflow
docs.text:
1. Load Sample Data      →  File > Import Data
2. Select Isotopes       →  Periodic table interface
3. Ionic Calibration     →  Sensitivity (counts → mass)
4. Transport Rate        →  Aerosol efficiency (3 methods)
5. Mass Fraction/Density →  Per-sample material properties
6. Detection Parameters  →  Method, confidence level, smoothing
7. Review in Canvas      →  Visualize and validate
8. Export               →  Summary + Details CSV

docs.title: Detection Methods
docs.text: Method Description Currie Method Classical detection based on Poisson statistics Formula C MARLAP-based, balances false positives/negatives Compound Poisson Log-Normal Advanced — accounts for signal distribution Manual User-defined threshold

docs.title: Supported Data Formats
docs.text:
• Nu Vitesse folder — directory containing run.info
  • TOFWERK .h5 — HDF5 acquisition files
  • CSV — Time-series (first column = Time in ms/ns/s; element columns as 107Ag, 56Fe, …)

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text:
Run.py
└── SplashCoordinator → ProgressiveMainWindow
    └── MainWindow
        ├── theme.py              (ThemeManager, palettes, QSS)
        ├── Project I/O           (fast_project_io, project_manager)
        ├── Peak Detection        (peak_detection, SIA_manager)
        ├── Calibration           (ionic_CAL, TE_*)
        └── Results Canvas        (canvas_widgets)
            ├── shared_plot_utils / shared_annotation
            └── results_*.py      (16 plot modules)

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: All notable changes to IsotopeTrack are documented here.

docs.title: New Features
docs.text:
• Particle filter added to the results
  • Isobaric correction module
  • Filter for non-linear peaks
  • Import multiple folders inside a main folder at once

docs.title: Improvements
docs.text:
• Added legend to network results figure
  • Cluster results now saved with project
  • "Results" button is highlighted when there are unsaved modifications
  • All application print() output replaced with structured logging (logs/*.jsonl)
  • Example datasets moved to a dedicated GitHub release, slimming the repository
  • Developer/build dependencies split into requirements-dev.txt
  • Documentation reference pages now auto-generated from source (docs_gen.py)

docs.title: Bug Fixes
docs.text:
• Fixed bugs with table parameters
  • Fixed bugs in results figures

docs.title: New Features
docs.text:
• CLI support — app can now be launched from terminal with arguments:
  • Load project files directly
  • Load Nu and TOFWERK data files
  • Select isotopes and presets via command line
  • See tools/cli_utils.py for details
  • Isobaric correction module (still in development)

docs.title: Improvements
docs.text:
• Improved Windows performance — replaced QTableWidget with QTableView in main window reducing lag significantly
  • Theme file moved to tools/ for better organization

docs.title: New Features
docs.text: • Cluster analysis added option to test all clustering methods at once

docs.title: Improvements
docs.text:
• Particle concentration per mL now reported in results figures
  • UI improvements in the main window

docs.title: v1.0.6 — 2026-05-30
docs.text:
New Features

Version checker app now automatically checks for newer versions on startup Cluster analysis new metric scores added

Improvements

Updated main window UI Standardized Results plot dialogs with a four-button UI contract (Plot format settings, Configure plot quantities, Reset layout, Export figure) across: Ternary plots, Correlation Matrix, Concentration, Network, Pie Chart, Heatmap, Single/Multiple Cleaned right-click menus to avoid duplicating bottom-button actions while preserving quick toggles and isotope label controls

Bug Fixes

Fixed memory leaks across multiple modules Fixed errors and bugs in the AI results module Fixed mass method — users can now select a new isotope after an initial selection Fixed cluster visualisation bugs Fixed requirements

docs.title: New Features
docs.text:
• Window titles — each window now displays its own name in the title bar
  • CPLN quantiles — implemented Compound Poisson Log-Normal quantiles from SPCal lookup table (cpln_quantiles.npz)
  • Detection method info — added additional information displayed in the detection method panel

docs.title: Bug Fixes
docs.text:
• Fixed main window: the background bug, when the user calculate the background using window size
  • Fixed bar plot display order — elements now appear in the correct order

docs.title: Internal
docs.text:
• Automated CI/CD pipeline for macOS and Windows builds
  • Added version.py to update version across all files in one command

docs.title: Improvements
docs.text:
• Integrated data points are now shown directly in the main window results
  • Updated cluster analysis with improved metrics

docs.title: Bug Fixes
docs.text: • Fixed several rendering and interaction bugs on the results canvas

docs.title: Main Window
docs.text: • Full light / dark theme toggle with live switching across all dialogs and widgets

docs.title: Single-Ion Analysis (SIA)
docs.text: • Detection parameters now configured independently per isotope

docs.title: Export
docs.text:
• CSV export supports multiple unit systems (ag, fg, pg, ng · amol, fmol, pmol · nm, µm)
  • Export includes additional analysis detection parameters

docs.title: Peak Detection
docs.text:
• Added integration threshold method
  • Added midpoint and watershed separation
  • Added Aiken iterative threshold method
  • Detection engine now uses Compound Poisson Log-Normal as the primary model
  • Implemented result caching — significantly reduces processing time on large datasets

docs.title: Signal Time Scan
docs.text: • Exclusion regions can now be defined per sample and per isotope

docs.title: Ionic Calibration
docs.text:
• Mass range mismatches detected and reported for Nu Vitesse data
  • Individual calibration points can be excluded interactively

docs.title: Transport Rate Calibration
docs.text: • All three calibration tabs unified in a single window

docs.title: Results Canvas
docs.text:
• Plot backends unified: Matplotlib and PyQtGraph for interactive plots
  • Clustering module improved with more control over distance metrics and linkage

docs.title: New Features
docs.text:
• Results Canvas completely redesigned with three new figures:
  • Network — multi-element particle relationships
  • Concentration — particle concentration overview
  • Matrix — element correlation matrix
  • Intel Mac support — dedicated native bundle for Intel-based Macs (x86_64)
  • Faster project saving and loading

docs.title: Bug Fixes
docs.text:
• Fixed multiple bugs in the Results Canvas display
  • Fixed several issues in the main window
  • Fixed Windows lag and slow response
